one_time_description: Write off of old accounts payable
economic_notes: Ndeh Limited Partnership is a government business enterprise accounted for under modified equity method, engaged in rental, contract work, hotel operations, firefighting and logging. Partnership reported net loss of $1,052,340 and comprehensive loss of $2,022,967 for year ended December 31, 2020.
notes: Auditor firm name not clearly identified - stated only as 'Chartered Professional Accountants' located in Leduc, Alberta. Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Ndeh Limited Partnership is accounted for under modified equity method (GBE) with year-end of December 31, 2020. Tangible capital assets net book value not extractable from available text (Schedule 1 referenced but not fully provided). Housing expenses combined Housing (Schedule 7) $1,874,330 and Housing Rentals (Schedule 11) $480,999. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $47,682,833 + FNIHB $7,941,679 + ESDC $1,019,025 + CMHC $77,136 + Province of Alberta $501,436 + BC Hydro $35,000 + FNDF $407,911 = $57,665,020 approximately. OSR includes gas sales, propane sales, timber, fireguard, rent, property taxes, interest, industry consultation, other revenue. Large COVID-related deferred revenue received in year ($12.6M). Accumulated surplus end of year per statement is $109,606,501.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
High ISC dependency (75.76%) — operations heavily reliant on federal transfers
